nodes.label: Budapest weight-based parking fee (BEV 2000, ICE 1800, eff. 2027-01-01)
nodes.rationale: Effective 2027-01-01: BEVs above 2000 kg and ICE/PHEV above 1800 kg pay double the Budapest resident parking fee. Two thresholds are needed because BEVs are structurally heavier than ICE at the same class.
nodes.norm_label: budapest weight-based parking fee (bev 2000, ice 1800, eff. 2027-01-01)

nodes.label: Polite fetching: cache-first + robots-aware + retry/backoff
nodes.rationale: Re-runs of the scraper must not hammer source sites. Single source-of-truth via gzipped raw cache keyed by sha1(url) means re-running the pipeline on the same day is a no-op for the network layer. urllib.robotparser is avoided because it mis-parses some valid real-world robots.txt (e.g. cars-data.com) that group many User-agent lines and then disallows everything; a custom longest-match matcher is used instead.

nodes.label: Solve-once Cloudflare bypass (clearance cookie reuse)
nodes.rationale: katalogus.hasznaltauto.hu sits behind a Cloudflare JS challenge. Solving per page costs ~15s of headless browser time. Strategy: solve the challenge ONCE with Scrapling StealthyFetcher, capture the cf_clearance cookie + user-agent, then fetch every subsequent page with curl_cffi impersonating Chrome — ~0.5s per page. Re-solve is triggered automatically on 403 / 'just a moment' body.

nodes.label: Variant fingerprinting for idempotent upserts
nodes.rationale: All writes converge on natural keys. A variant fingerprint is the pipe-joined tuple (make, model, powertrain_subtype, power_kw, drivetrain, battery_kwh-bucket). Trims are appended for cars-data so distinct trims of the same engine are not collapsed. Re-scraping the same trim never creates a duplicate row; COALESCE in the ON CONFLICT clause means new non-null fields fill in gaps without overwriting existing non-null values.

nodes.label: ±2.5% weight tolerance for HU-catalog cross-check
nodes.rationale: cars-data.com and katalogus.hasznaltauto.hu often report slightly different curb weights for the same variant (different test conditions, equipment levels, DIN vs EG). A 2.5% delta is treated as agreement so the app can show a 'katalogus.hu confirms' badge rather than a 'conflict' warning. ±25% is treated as conflict, >25% would suggest a fundamentally different variant.

nodes.label: v2 API mirrors canonical app/fees classifier
nodes.rationale: Android Kotlin FeeClassifier and the FastAPI app/fees.classify are the source of truth for the parking-fee rule (over→double, straddle→borderline, under→ok, unknown→unknown, strict-inequality at threshold). The v2 SPA is a thin view, so the same rules are re-implemented server-side in v2_api._classify_row; pipeline/derive.py re-implements them again for the offline batch. This avoids drifting client/server/pipeline behaviour at the cost of a bit of duplication.
nodes.norm_label: v2 api mirrors canonical app/fees classifier

nodes.label: Dual-UI mounting via custom ASGI wrapper
nodes.rationale: FastAPI's StaticFiles cannot mount two SPA roots that share the '/' parent path. A tiny ASGI wrapper inspects scope['path'] and routes /v2/* to web/v2/ (with the prefix stripped so StaticFiles resolves correctly) and everything else to web/ (legacy SPA), so the existing StaticFiles mounts don't fight for the same paths.
nodes.norm_label: dual-ui mounting via custom asgi wrapper
